quickstart.title: Quick Start Guide
quickstart.description: Welcome to PolarAgent. This guide helps you get the core workflow running quickly.

blocks.text: Before using PolarAgent, configure at least one model service provider.

items: Click **Settings** at the bottom of the left sidebar.
Open **Model Settings** from the settings sidebar.
Click **Add Service** in the upper-right corner.
Choose a provider such as OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or a compatible local service.
Fill in the required fields: API Key, Base URL, and model name.
Click **Save**.
Make sure the provider card is enabled.

blocks.text: If you do not have an API key yet, register with the provider first. Common options include OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google AI Studio. Many providers offer trial credits.
Adjust the application to match your preferences and workflow.

items: **Theme**: choose light, dark, or system mode.
**Chat font**: choose sans, serif, or monospace.
**Chat size**: choose small, medium, large, or extra large.
**Voice input**: configure auto-send and text refinement.
Configure an image generation provider such as OpenAI DALL-E or Google Gemini.
Enter the required API key and parameters.
Set default image size and quality.
**Speech recognition (ASR)**: configure Whisper, Azure Speech, or another provider.
**Text to speech (TTS)**: configure a speech synthesis service.
Enter API keys and choose voice models.
Configure Brave Search, Exa, or SearXNG.
Enter an API key or server address.
Allow AI to search the web for up-to-date information.
Configure document vectorization with OpenAI Embeddings, local models, or another provider.
Embeddings power semantic search in knowledge bases.

blocks.text: After a model is configured, you can start talking with AI.

items: Click **New Chat** at the top of the left sidebar.
Type your question or request in the input box at the bottom of the main page.
Press **Enter** to send the message.
The assistant will respond; you can continue with follow-up questions or refinements.

blocks.text: Hold **Shift + Enter** to insert a line break instead of sending the message.
PolarAgent supports multiple agent roles with different specialties and capabilities.

items: Expand **Extensions** in the left sidebar.
Open **Agents**.
Browse available agent cards and read their capabilities.
Click **Start Chat** on an agent card to create a chat with that agent.

blocks.text: You can also choose an agent from the dropdown on the main chat page.
The left sidebar shows all of your chat history.

items: Click any chat title to switch to that chat.
Right-click a chat to rename, clear, or delete it.
Use the search button in the top title bar to quickly find previous conversations.

blocks.text: These shortcuts make daily use faster.

items: **Enter**: send the message.
**Shift + Enter**: insert a line break.
**Escape**: stop AI generation.

blocks.text: Top-bar buttons also provide quick access to search, sidebar toggles, and other actions.
After the quick start, continue with the feature guides.

items: Read **Chat** for conversation tips.
Explore **Skills** and **Tool Integrations** to extend AI capabilities.
Try **Team Collaboration** for multi-agent work.
Configure advanced features such as **Browser Use** and **Computer Use**.
Read **Tips** for more efficient workflows.

blocks.text: If you run into issues, check the **FAQ** section.

chat.title: Chat
chat.description: Learn how to communicate with AI assistants efficiently.

blocks.text: PolarAgent supports multiple parallel chats, each with its own saved context.

items: Click **New Chat** at the top of the left sidebar.
Click any chat title in the sidebar to switch.
Each chat automatically receives a title based on its content.

blocks.text: Use these input behaviors to work faster.

items: Press **Enter** to send.
Press **Shift + Enter** to insert a line break.
Click the send button beside the input box.
The input box grows automatically for multi-line text.

blocks.text: The toolbar below the input can add attachments, reference knowledge bases, and switch permission modes.
If a reply is going in the wrong direction or taking too long, interrupt it anytime.

items: Press **Escape** to stop immediately.
Or click the **Stop** button beside the input.
After stopping, edit your prompt and send again.

blocks.text: Right-click a chat in the sidebar to manage it.

items: **Rename**: change the chat title.
**Clear chat**: delete all messages but keep the session.
**Delete chat**: permanently remove the session.

blocks.text: Clearing a chat resets its context. Deleting a chat removes the whole session.

items: Click the search icon in the top title bar.
Search chat titles and message content by keyword.
Click a result to jump to the matching chat.
Hover a message and click **Copy** in the upper-right corner.
Code blocks have a separate **Copy code** button.
A copied confirmation appears after success.

blocks.text: After setting a working directory, AI can work with files in that folder when tools allow it.

items: Click **Choose working directory** near the bottom of the main page.
Select a local folder.
New chats will use that directory.

blocks.text: Working directories are useful for code projects, document cleanup, and file-oriented tasks.

items: **Ask every time**: confirm before each tool action.
**Auto allow**: trusted tools can run without confirmation.
**Always deny**: block all tool usage.

blocks.text: Switch permission mode from the toolbar below the input.

items: Avoid mixing too many unrelated topics in one chat.
For very long chats, consider clearing history or starting a new chat.
Use **Clear chat** to reset context while keeping the session.
Keep important chats instead of deleting them casually.

blocks.text: Confused, repetitive, or irrelevant replies often mean the context is too long or messy. Start fresh or clear the chat.

items: Click the microphone icon beside the input.
Start speaking; speech will be converted to text.
Click the microphone again to stop recording.

blocks.text: Voice input requires a speech recognition provider such as Whisper or Azure to be configured in settings.

agent.title: Agent Management
agent.description: Learn how to create and manage AI agents with different roles.

blocks.text: An agent is an AI persona with a specific role and capability set.

items: **Avatar and name**: identify the agent.
**Description**: explain what the agent is good at.
**System prompt**: define identity, behavior, and answer style.
**Model configuration**: choose provider and model.
**Enabled skills**: choose which skills the agent can use.
Expand **Extensions** in the left sidebar.
Click **Agents**.
Use the top tabs: Agent Square, Built-in Agents, and Custom Agents.
**Agent Square**: browse and install community agents.
**Built-in Agents**: preinstalled agents that can be edited but not deleted.
**Custom Agents**: agents you create and fully control.
Browse agent cards.
Review names, descriptions, and capability notes.
Click **Start Chat** to create a new session with that agent.
Or select an agent from the dropdown on the main page.

blocks.text: Different agents fit different situations: coding agents for code, writing agents for content, and general agents for everyday Q&A.

items: Switch to **Custom Agents**.
Click **+ Create**.
Configure the agent in the editor.
Click **Save**.
Click the avatar button to choose an emoji.
Enter a clear agent name such as Python Tutor or Copywriting Assistant.
Keep the name short and recognizable.
Briefly describe the agent's purpose and strengths.
Help yourself and other users understand when to choose it.
Example: Focused on Python teaching and code optimization.
**Model service**: follow model settings or choose a specific provider.
**Model**: choose a specific model such as GPT-4 or Claude.
Leave the model empty to follow the default routing model.

blocks.text: For most agents, following model settings is enough. Configure a specific model only when the task needs that model's strengths.

items: Define the agent's **identity and role** clearly.
Describe **boundaries**: what it should and should not do.
Specify **answer style**: formal or casual, concise or detailed.
Define a **tool-use strategy**: when to act and when to ask.
Include background knowledge and examples when useful.

blocks.text: Example: You are an experienced Python tutor. Explain concepts clearly, provide runnable examples, include necessary comments, prefer the standard library, and avoid unnecessary dependencies.

items: Turn on **All skills** to let the agent use every current and future skill.
Or select specific skills for tighter control.
Each skill includes a name and description.
Enabled skills appear selected.

blocks.text: If you are unsure which skills are needed, enable all skills and let AI choose when appropriate.

items: Click the gear icon on an agent card to open the editor.
Click **Save** after changing settings.
New chats use the updated configuration; existing chats keep their current context.
Click the trash icon to delete a custom agent.
Built-in agents can be edited but not deleted.
Switch to **Agent Square**.
Browse recommended agents and review descriptions and ratings.
Click **Install**.
Installed agents appear under Custom Agents.
Choose an agent from the dropdown on the main page.
Future **New Chat** actions will use that agent automatically.
You can switch to another agent anytime from the dropdown.
**Clear responsibility**: one agent should do one thing well.
**Specific instructions**: avoid vague prompts.
**Examples**: include desired output examples.
**Boundaries**: make expertise and limits explicit.
**Iterate**: adjust prompts based on real results.
A **model** is the underlying AI engine.
An **agent** is a role customization built on top of a model.
Multiple agents can share one model with different system prompts.
Agents can use a dedicated model or the default model.

skill.title: Skills
skill.description: Learn how to use and manage extension skills for AI.

items: **Prebuilt capability**: encapsulates task-specific logic.
**Plug and play**: enable or disable without restarting.
**Composable**: combine multiple skills for complex work.
**Upgradeable**: update skills independently.
Open **Skills** from the left navigation.
Browse installed skills and the skill store.
Click a skill card for details and examples.
Switch to the **Skill Store** tab.
Search or browse for the skill you need.
Click **Install** and wait for download and setup.
Installed skills are enabled automatically.

blocks.text: Start with official skills first. They are tested and generally more stable.

items: AI can automatically choose suitable skills based on your request.
You can explicitly ask: Use the XXX skill to help me...
Skill execution shows progress and status.
Completed skills return results and details.
**Data processing**: file operations, conversion, analysis.
**Network requests**: API calls, scraping, data retrieval.
**Code execution**: run code, execute scripts, manage environments.
**Media**: image processing, audio conversion, video editing.
**Office automation**: document generation, spreadsheets, email.
Enable or disable installed skills.
Disabled skills will not be called in chats.
Update skills to newer versions.
Uninstall skills you no longer need.

blocks.text: If a skill is triggered too often by mistake, disable it temporarily and re-enable it when needed.

items: **File system**: read and write local files and folders.
**Network access**: access the internet and external APIs.
**System commands**: execute commands and scripts.

blocks.text: When a skill needs permission for the first time, read the permission details before granting access.

tool.title: Tool Integrations
tool.description: Learn how to integrate external tools that extend AI capabilities.

items: **API integrations**: connect third-party services and APIs.
**Automation**: run system commands or scripts.
**Data sources**: access databases, files, and other systems.
View configured tools.
Check tool status and usage statistics.
Test whether a tool connection works.
Provide API keys or access tokens.
Set service endpoint URLs.
Configure authentication methods and parameters.

blocks.text: Protect API keys and other secrets. Do not paste or share them directly in chats.

items: **Search engines**: web and academic search.
**Code repositories**: GitHub and GitLab integrations.
**Cloud services**: storage and compute platforms.
**Databases**: SQL and NoSQL connections.

team.title: Team Collaboration
team.description: Learn how to let multiple AI agents work together.

items: **Multi-agent collaboration**: agents with different strengths solve problems together.
**Task decomposition**: subtasks are assigned by capability.
**Shared context**: members share context and intermediate results.
**Deliberation**: multiple perspectives analyze and validate a plan.
Open **Teams** and click **Create Team**.
Name the team and add a description.
Add at least two members.
Assign roles and responsibilities.
Configure serial, parallel, or hybrid collaboration mode.
Select a team from the main page or sidebar.
Describe your task or question.
The team starts collaborating and divides the work.
Review each member's progress.
Receive a combined final result and suggestions.

blocks.text: Teams are useful for tasks that need multiple specialties, such as software development or content production.

items: **Serial**: members work in order; later members depend on earlier output.
**Parallel**: members work independently at the same time.
**Hybrid**: the task selects serial or parallel flow automatically.
Adjust members and responsibilities anytime.
Review historical collaboration records.
Export team reports.
Archive or delete teams you no longer need.
**Software development**: product manager + architect + developer + tester.
**Content creation**: planner + writer + editor + proofreader.
**Data analysis**: collection + cleaning + analysis + visualization.
**Decision support**: multi-angle analysis + risk assessment + option comparison.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.description: Learn how to let AI use your documents and domain knowledge.

items: **Document indexing**: analyze and index uploaded documents.
**Semantic search**: search by intent, not only keywords.
**Context enhancement**: add relevant knowledge to replies.
**Live updates**: new documents become usable after indexing.
Open **Knowledge Base** and click **Create Knowledge Base**.
Name it and add a description.
Choose an embedding model.
Upload documents or add text content.
Wait for indexing to complete.
**Text documents**: TXT, MD, PDF, DOCX.
**Code files**: source code in many languages.
**Data files**: CSV, JSON, YAML.
**Web content**: HTML or fetched URLs.

blocks.text: Put related documents for the same project or topic into one knowledge base so AI can understand context relationships.

items: Select a related knowledge base when creating a chat.
AI retrieves relevant content automatically.
You can ask explicitly: answer based on the knowledge base.
Referenced content is marked with sources in the reply.
Open Settings > Embeddings.
Choose an embedding provider such as OpenAI or a local model.
Enter the API key or model path.
Test the connection.

blocks.text: Local embedding models avoid API fees but need stronger hardware. Online services are faster but cost money.

items: Add or delete documents anytime.
Re-index documents after updates.
View usage statistics.
Export content and indexes.
Keep documents clearly structured with headings.
Avoid duplicate or outdated content.
Clean up unneeded documents regularly.
Split large documents into smaller files.

model.title: Model Settings
model.description: Learn how to configure and manage AI model providers.

items: **OpenAI**: GPT model families.
**Anthropic**: Claude models.
**Google**: Gemini models.
**Local models**: Ollama, LM Studio, and similar deployments.
**Other**: custom services compatible with the OpenAI API format.
Open Settings > Model Settings.
Click **Add Provider**.
Choose a provider type.
Enter the API key and required settings.
Click **Test connection**.
Save and use it in chats.

blocks.text: Configure multiple providers as backups so you can switch quickly if one service has issues.

items: Install and start Ollama or another local service.
Download the model you need.
Add a **Local Model** provider in PolarAgent.
Set the service URL, usually `http://localhost:11434`.
Choose the model name.

blocks.text: Local models do not require network access or API fees, but they need capable hardware and are often slower than hosted services.

items: Click **Set as default** on a model card.
New chats use the default model automatically.
You can switch models inside a chat anytime.
**GPT-4**: strong general reasoning and coding.
**GPT-3.5**: fast responses for simple Q&A.
**Claude**: strong long-context understanding and writing.
**Gemini**: strong multimodal support including image understanding.
Use smaller models for simple tasks.
Use larger models only for complex work.
Avoid unnecessarily long context; clear history when appropriate.
Use local models for high-frequency tasks when hardware allows.
Keep API keys private.
Rotate keys regularly.
Set spending limits to avoid unexpected usage.
Monitor usage in the provider dashboard.

browseruse.title: Browser Use
browseruse.description: Control a real Chrome browser through an extension so AI can operate web pages.

items: **Real browser environment**: operate inside Chrome with login state and cookies.
**Automation**: click, type, scroll, and capture screenshots.
**Web interaction**: fill forms, browse sites, and extract information.
**Persistent sessions**: login state can remain across chats.
Click **Settings** in the left sidebar.
Open **Advanced** > **Browser Use**.
Find the browser extension installation card.
Click **Export extension to folder**.
Choose a convenient location such as Desktop or Documents.
Remember the exported folder path named PolarAgent-BrowserUse.

blocks.text: The exported folder contains all extension files. Do not modify or delete its contents.
Step 2: Load the extension in Chrome

items: Open Chrome.
Go to `chrome://extensions`.
Enable **Developer mode** in the upper-right corner.
Click **Load unpacked**.
Select the exported **PolarAgent-BrowserUse** folder.
Click **Select folder** to finish installation.

blocks.text: If Chrome warns that developer mode extensions are disabled, choose to keep or continue using the extension. This is expected.

items: After installation, the PolarAgent icon appears in Chrome's extension area.
Return to Browser Use settings in PolarAgent.
The status card should show **Connected**.
Available tabs should show the number of open tabs.

blocks.text: If the status is not connected, refresh Chrome tabs or restart PolarAgent.

items: Create a new chat.
Describe the browser task, such as: open GitHub and search TypeScript projects; check the weather website; fill in this form.
AI calls Browser Use automatically.
You can watch the operation in Chrome in real time.
**WebSocket port**: local extension connection port, default 18765.
**HTTP API port**: API service port, default 18767.
**Operation timeout**: maximum wait for one action, default 30 seconds.
**Post-action delay**: delay after each action, default 300 ms.
**Verbose logs**: output more debugging information.

blocks.text: Default settings are usually enough. Adjust them only for troubleshooting or special optimization.

items: **Information gathering**: visit sites and extract specific data.
**Form filling**: automate repeated web forms.
**Web testing**: test whether interactive behavior works.
**Monitoring**: periodically check page changes.
**Data collection**: collect data across multiple pages.
Browser Use controls a real browser and can change browser state.
Use permission prompts for sensitive actions such as payment or deletion.
Some websites may detect automation and limit access.
Keep Chrome and PolarAgent running at the same time.
Do not manually control the same tab while AI is operating it.

blocks.text: Q: The extension is installed but not connected.

items: Confirm PolarAgent is running.
Check the WebSocket port.
Refresh Chrome tabs.
Restart PolarAgent or Chrome.

blocks.text: Q: AI cannot operate some web elements.

items: Dynamic content may need more waiting time.
Complex JavaScript interactions may not be supported.
Ask AI to try a different targeting method.

blocks.text: Q: Can it control multiple browser windows?
Yes. Browser Use manages open tabs and can switch between them.

computeruse.title: Computer Use
computeruse.description: Let AI control your computer with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and screen operations.

items: **Screen control**: AI can inspect the screen and locate elements.
**Mouse actions**: click, double-click, drag, and more.
**Keyboard input**: type text and use shortcuts.
**Screenshot analysis**: capture and understand the interface.

blocks.text: Computer Use is powerful and risky. Stay attentive and avoid letting AI perform sensitive actions.

items: Click **Settings** in the left sidebar.
Open **Advanced** > **Computer Use**.
Review and adjust settings.
**Persistent worker**: keep the background process running for faster response.
**Max search depth**: UI element search depth, default 5.
**Max node count**: number of UI elements analyzed at once, default 250.
**Screenshot mode**: choose storage and transfer behavior.
**Restore clipboard**: restore clipboard content after operations.
**Operation timeout**: maximum wait for one action, default 60 seconds.
Create a new chat.
Describe an operation, such as: open Notepad and type text; capture and analyze the screen; click the button in the upper-left corner.
AI requests Computer Use permission.
After you approve, AI performs the operation.

blocks.text: For first use, choose **Ask every time** so every action requires confirmation.

items: **Automation**: repeat interface operations.
**Screenshot analysis**: let AI inspect and understand the screen.
**Software testing**: test UI behavior.
**Demo recording**: perform demo steps automatically.
**Data entry**: fill forms or enter data in batches.
**Permissions**: use Ask every time to prevent mistakes.
**Sensitive actions**: be extra careful with deletion, system settings, and payments.
**Supervision**: watch while AI operates and be ready to stop.
**Backups**: back up important data before risky operations.
**Privacy**: screenshots may contain sensitive information.

blocks.text: Computer Use sends screenshots to the AI model for analysis. Avoid it when passwords, personal data, or other sensitive information are visible.

items: Operation speed depends on AI reasoning and is not suited to real-time tasks.
Complex interfaces may be misidentified.
Some apps block automation.
Multi-monitor setups may cause positioning errors.
Frequent use consumes more AI tokens because screenshots are sent.
Split complex tasks into simple steps.
Describe UI positions clearly.
Use it on simple, familiar interfaces first.
Test in a safe environment before using it for real work.
Keep window focus stable while AI is operating.

blocks.text: Q: AI cannot find an element on screen.

items: Provide a more detailed position description.
Make sure the target is visible and unobstructed.
Resize the window or adjust layout.
Describe color, text, or other visual features.

blocks.text: Q: An operation fails or times out.

items: Check that the application is responsive.
Increase operation timeout.
Split complex actions into smaller steps.
Make sure no modal blocks the operation.

blocks.text: Q: What is the difference from Browser Use?

Browser Use is specialized for browser automation and can access web page DOM, making it more precise. Computer Use is general desktop automation based on visual recognition, so it works with any app but is slower and less accurate. Prefer Browser Use for web tasks.

tips.title: Tips
tips.description: Use these techniques to work more efficiently in PolarAgent.

items: **Enter**: send message.
**Shift + Enter**: line break.
**Escape**: stop AI generation.

blocks.text: More actions are available from the top buttons, including search, sidebar toggle, and tutorials.

items: **Be specific**: describe the result you want.
**Provide context**: include background and constraints.
**Break it down**: split complex tasks into smaller questions.
**Request a format**: table, code, list, or another shape.
**Provide examples**: show the style or output you expect.

blocks.text: Example: instead of write some code, ask for a Python function that takes two numbers, returns their sum, and includes error handling.

items: Ask for comments when needed.
Ask for exception handling and edge cases.
Ask for unit tests.
Ask for performance improvements and explanations.
First round: get a basic implementation.
Second round: point out issues and request improvements.
Third round: add edge cases and optimization.
Fourth round: polish documentation and comments.
AI remembers the current conversation.
Refer to earlier work: based on the code above...
Ask for comparisons: how is this different from the previous approach?

blocks.text: When a chat gets very long, AI may forget early details. Summarize key points and start a new chat.

items: **Programming**: specify language, framework, versions, and errors.
**Writing**: specify style, audience, length, and outline.
**Learning**: explain your current level and ask for examples.
**Analysis**: provide complete data and analysis dimensions.
Give each team member a clear role.
Let members focus on their strengths.
Use serial mode for complex tasks that need coherence.
Use parallel mode for simple independent subtasks.
Use clear section headings.
Place important concepts in separate paragraphs.
Avoid overly long documents; under 10,000 words is a good target.
Put related documents in the same knowledge base.

blocks.text: Q: The API key is configured but still does not work.

items: Check for copied spaces before or after the key.
Confirm the key is valid and has balance or quota.
Check network connectivity.
Use **Test connection** to see the exact error.
Confirm Ollama or another local service is running.
Check the service URL, usually `http://localhost:11434`.
Confirm the model has finished downloading.
Check whether the firewall blocks the connection.
Check network speed.
Try another model provider.
Clear or restart when chat history is too long.
Local models need sufficient hardware.

blocks.text: Q: Replies are inaccurate or off-topic.

items: Describe the problem and goal more clearly.
Provide more context.
Check whether the selected agent fits the task.
Start fresh if the history is too long.

blocks.text: Q: A chat interrupts or errors.

items: Check API balance.
Content safety may have been triggered; rephrase the request.
Very long replies may time out; ask for segmented output.
Read the error details and follow the hint.

blocks.text: Q: How can AI access local files?

items: Create a knowledge base and upload files.
Or paste or describe file contents in the chat.
Use a skill with file access permission.

blocks.text: For safety, AI cannot freely access your file system. Provide content through knowledge bases, attachments, or approved tools.

items: Check network connectivity.
Confirm enough storage is available.
Restart the app and try again.
Check logs for the specific error.

blocks.text: Q: Team collaboration is not effective.

items: Make member roles clearer.
Adjust collaboration mode.
Describe the task more precisely.
Change the team composition if needed.
Close chats and teams you do not need.
Clean old chat history regularly.
Reduce the number of simultaneous tasks.
Check system resource usage.

blocks.text: Q: Storage usage is too high.

items: Delete unneeded chat history.
Clean outdated knowledge base documents.
Uninstall unused skills.
Check storage details in settings.
API keys are encrypted and stored locally.
They are not uploaded to PolarAgent servers.
Rotate keys regularly.
Never send keys directly in chats.
Chat records are stored locally on your device.
They are not uploaded to PolarAgent servers.
They are sent to the model provider you configure when generating replies.
Do not send sensitive information in chats.
Check application logs for detailed errors.
Click **Reset to defaults** in settings.
Open an issue in the GitHub repository.
Ask the community for help.

blocks.text: When reporting a problem, include error details, reproduction steps, and screenshots so others can help faster.
